member.name: James Cartlidge
member.synopsis: James Cartlidge is the Conservative MP for South Suffolk , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. He currently undertakes the role of Shadow Secretary of State for Defence.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: CASOM
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the out of service date for Storm Shadow.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I am withholding this information for reasons of operational security.

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what size of workforce will be required to build the (a) Type 91, (b) Type 92, (c) Type 93, (d) Type 94 and (e) Common Combat Vessel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan set out the Royal Navy’s future approach to a family of autonomous and hybrid naval capabilities, including the Type 91, Type 92, Type 93 and Type 94 systems, alongside the Common Combat Vessel. Construction schedules and workf...

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what is the construction timeline of the (a) Type 91, (b) Type 92, (c) Type 93, (d) Type 94 and (e) Common Combat Vessel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan set out the Royal Navy’s future approach to a family of autonomous and hybrid naval capabilities, including the Type 91, Type 92, Type 93 and Type 94 systems, alongside the Common Combat Vessel. Construction schedules and workf...

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much of the £1bn to be spent on the Royal Navy's hybrid over the next four years will be spent on the (a) Type 91, (b) Type 92, (c) Type 93, (d) Type 94 and (e) Common Combat Vessel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defence will invest at least £1.5 billion over the next four years to begin delivering the Hybrid Navy, including the integration of autonomous underwater, surface and airborne systems to deliver mass and persistence. By 2030, we aim to have brought the f...

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many contracts have been issued as part of the Defence Investment Plan as of 1 July 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan (DIP), published on 30 June 2026, sets out the Ministry of Defence's investment priorities through to 2029-30. The publication of the Plan followed significant preparatory work to prioritise investment, develop programmes and p...

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, if he will state the confirmed funding allocated for the Defence Investment Plan.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to my response to his Question 10206 of 16 June 2025.https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-16/10206

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Guided Weapons
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what types of missile will be carried on the Type 91.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan announced the development of the Type 91 uncrewed missile platform as part of the Royal Navy's future maritime integrated air and missile defence and strike capability. The number of platforms to be procured has not yet been de...

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Guided Weapons
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many missiles will be carried by a single Type 91 vessel.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan announced the development of the Type 91 uncrewed missile platform as part of the Royal Navy's future maritime integrated air and missile defence and strike capability. The number of platforms to be procured has not yet been de...

writtenQuestions.heading: Warships: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how many Type 91s will be procured.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan announced the development of the Type 91 uncrewed missile platform as part of the Royal Navy's future maritime integrated air and missile defence and strike capability. The number of platforms to be procured has not yet been de...

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Cadets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether he has authorised a reduction in the funding for cadets in the (a) current and (b) next Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not allocate funding directly to the Cadet Forces. The cost of delivering and supporting Cadet activity falls across a wide range of business areas, agencies, and organisations, and there are no discreet Cadet budgets as such....

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Cadets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much was spent on cadet funding in financial year (a) 2024-25 and (b) 2025-26.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not allocate funding directly to the Cadet Forces. The cost of delivering and supporting Cadet activity falls across a wide range of business areas, agencies, and organisations, and there are no discreet Cadet budgets as such....

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Cadets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much will be spent on cadet funding in the (a) each remaining financial year of the current Parliament and (b) each financial year of the next Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence does not allocate funding directly to the Cadet Forces. The cost of delivering and supporting Cadet activity falls across a wide range of business areas, agencies, and organisations, and there are no discreet Cadet budgets as such....

writtenQuestions.heading: Skynet
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the capability gap created by his Department's decision to cancel the Skynet 6 programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Skynet 6 programme has not been cancelled. There is continued investment in the delivery of the next generation of systems to support satellite communications out to the 2040s. As reported in the Defence Investment Plan, there has been a change in app...

writtenQuestions.heading: NATO
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the Defence Investment Plan on the United Kingdom's abilities to fulfil its NATO commitments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan (DIP) implements our ‘NATO First’ approach. It places NATO requirements and interoperability at the heart of UK capability planning and has been developed to deliver the optimal UK contribution into NATO’s warfighting plans. We...

writtenQuestions.heading: Uncrewed Systems: Testing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the regulatory frameworks on the testing of uncrewed and autonomous systems.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence launched a review of the regulatory system governing uncrewed and autonomous systems in defence in February 2026, inviting contributions from industry and the wider public. Defence is committed to reforming regulation to ease the b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Uncrewed Systems: Testing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the regulatory approval process on the testing of uncrewed and autonomous systems.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Defence launched a review of the regulatory system governing uncrewed and autonomous systems in defence in February 2026, inviting contributions from industry and the wider public. Defence is committed to reforming regulation to ease the b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Uncrewed Systems: Testing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, how much will his Department invest in autonomy testing infrastructure in the United Kingdom in the (a) current Parliament and (b) next Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Autonomous capability is at the forefront of Uncrewed Systems development across air, land, maritime and sub-surface domains. Ministry of Defence will optimise use of the Test, Trials, Training & Evaluation partnership with QinetiQ backed by a £1.54 b...

writtenQuestions.heading: Global Combat Air Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether he has a target date for the first flight trial of the Global Combat Air Programme prototype.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The exact date for the first flight will be finalised nearer the time to leverage maximum benefit to Global Combat Air Programme development.

writtenQuestions.heading: Type 83 Destroyers: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, pursuant to the Answer of 27 January to Question 106653 on Type 83 Destroyers: Procurement, when the review of the Type 83 concept will conclude.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan sets out the Royal Navy’s (RN) approach to delivering the UK’s most advanced maritime air defence capability and the rationale for this approach. Rather than delivering this capability through a small number of large and expens...

writtenQuestions.heading: Type 83 Destroyers: Procurement
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what his planned timetable is for delivering the Type 83 programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Defence Investment Plan sets out the Royal Navy’s (RN) approach to delivering the UK’s most advanced maritime air defence capability and the rationale for this approach. Rather than delivering this capability through a small number of large and expens...
